findings.title: resume load 時 origin anchor 不在ケースの挙動未定義（初回 checkpoint 前クラッシュ）
findings.rationale: R3 は 'resume load 時: on-disk journal が origin anchor の digest と一致すること' と規定するが、`commitFinalState` checkpoint push がまだ一度も発生していない場合（job が awaiting-resume に達する前にクラッシュ）、origin に anchor が存在しない。このケースで fail-open（スキップ）か fail-closed（resume 拒否）かが未定義。T4 の受け入れ基準は暗黙的に 'checkpoint push 済みジョブ' を前提としているが、request.md に明記されていない。spec step でこの境界を決定して記録しなければ、実装時に設計ドリフトが生じる。

observations.title: コード前提の事実確認: 全アサーション検証済み
observations.rationale: request.md §現状コードの前提 に記載されたすべてのファイル:行/シンボル/パス を実コードで確認した。executor.ts:405 output-contract gate, :436 finalizeStepArtifacts block, :461-466 captureHeadSha; commit-push.ts:48 git add -A, :105-146 commitFinalState/checkpoint; round-git-scope.ts:54-55 pipelineManagedPaths; verify-checkpoint.ts self-consistency のみ; loadSplitLayout mid-journal corruption のみ; ADR-20260717/ADR-20260718 は architecture/adr/ に存在し accepted — すべて事実と一致。

observations.title: 設計判断セクションと ADR-20260718 D1-D6 の整合確認
observations.rationale: request.md の 'architect 評価済みの設計判断' セクションは ADR-20260718 D1（不変）・D2（authorship 境界）・D3（committed-tree anchor）・D4（resume/attach 再検証）・D5（復元+halt）と完全に対応している。要件 R1-R5 も D2-D5 を 1:1 で実装している。スコープ外（sandbox/#783/D2-OS 層・#464 一般 denylist）の除外理由も ADR と一貫。

toolResult.reason: Wrote design.md, spec.md, and tasks.md under specrunner/changes/evidence-authorship-enforcement/ on branch change/evidence-authorship-enforcement-bb29f8b1. The plan implements ADR-20260718's authorship boundary via detection+restoration on all three named paths (per-node commit, resume load, attach): (D3) sequential per-node commit excludes pipelineManagedPaths so agent code commits never carry the journal; (D1/D2) an in-process pipeline-authored anchor tracked at the single JobJournal.persist chokepoint; (D4) a durable, agent-unreachable, crash-surviving origin digest ref computed from the in-process anchor as the independent authenticity witness; (D5) per-node verification (committed-tree ∩ journal empty + on-disk digest == anchor) between produce and apply, fail-closed; (D6) resume-load verification with restore-from-origin; (D7) attach authenticity predicate in verify-checkpoint (reject, no local state); (D8) checkpoint publish restores trusted bytes before push. tasks.md maps T1–T8 acceptance criteria to granular tasks with adversarial negatives. No source files edited (planning only). Notable design judgments surfaced as decisions/open-questions: durable ref updated on every pipeline journal push (strengthening the request's "at checkpoint push" for exact-match resume/attach), and round-member per-step verification left out of scope with rationale.

findings.title: In-process anchor not seeded for resumed processes — per-node on-disk check breaks on every resumed first-node
findings.rationale: T-06 check (b) compares SHA256 of the full on-disk events.jsonl against the in-process anchor. For a fresh run the anchor accumulates from empty and always matches the full file. For a resumed process, JobJournal.persist finds existingCounters on disk and appends only the delta records; the in-process registry starts empty and accumulates only delta bytes. After begin(), anchor = delta-only, on-disk = full history from all sessions. SHA256(full) ≠ SHA256(delta) → check (b) always fails for the first resumed node (false positive, violates T6). Alternatively if check (b) is skipped when anchor is delta-only, T1/T2 tampers during the first resumed node go undetected. D2's 'defers to durable anchor' does not resolve this: after begin() appends new records, SHA256(on-disk) > SHA256(durable anchor), so durable-anchor comparison also fails. Neither interpretation satisfies both T1 and T6 for resumed processes.

findings.title: Pipeline journal commit placement relative to commitSuccess leaves success-era bytes durably unanchored per-node
findings.rationale: T-04 says 'wire the pipeline-managed journal commit after per-node verification passes'; T-06 says verification runs 'between produce and apply'. This places the pipeline journal push before orchestrator.apply() → commitSuccess(). But commitSuccess appends verdict-history and success-persist records that are not included in the per-node push — only pushed at the next node or checkpoint. The Risks section accepts this crash window, but the tasks do not state this ordering explicitly. An implementer may try to include success-era bytes in the per-node push, which would require the orchestrator (B-13 single-writer) to perform a git side effect, violating B-13.

findings.title: Two Open Questions unresolved: durable ref object form and round-path journal commit sweep
findings.rationale: The Open Questions section leaves (a) the durable ref's git object type (blob vs tag vs commit) and exact namespace unspecified, and (b) whether the coordinator round needs its own post-round journal commit. If the final pipeline step is a round and there is no subsequent sequential node, origin may be missing round-era journal bytes at checkpoint push time, leaving the durable anchor incomplete. Both must be resolved before implementation.

findings.title: T6 'clean resume' scope ambiguous relative to accepted crash-path spurious-halt posture
findings.rationale: T6 requires no authenticity halt on 'a clean resume', while the Risks section simultaneously accepts that a crash between a per-node journal push and the next commitSuccess causes a spurious halt on resume (accepted P0 posture). These are only consistent if 'clean resume' means 'resume from intentional awaiting-resume pause', not 'resume after any crash'. The distinction is not written down, so test authors may incorrectly expect crash-recovery resumes to pass T6.

findings.title: T-06 fail-closed rule contradicts D2 fail-open for absent anchor
findings.rationale: T-06 says 'absent anchor-with-present-on-disk that cannot be evaluated is a violation'; D2 says 'absence preserves current behavior (no check)'. These conflict unless 'cannot be evaluated' is narrowly scoped to mean BOTH in-process AND durable are absent. The current phrasing allows opposing implementations.

message: spec-review iteration 1 completed with verdict: escalation
